title: eryngium humboldtii
intro: Eryngium humboldtii, also known as Humboldt's eryngo or sea eryngo, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits also include its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating skin conditions and wounds. Eryngium humboldtii can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
